story: After weeks tracking an Arak, the hunter Waxman reached its nest: a shallow cup of kirrel grass holding many dazzling eggs. He forced himself to choose carefully, not by beauty alone. A tremor of air warned him to keep low. Crawling, his hand found an ivory egg blushed rose, a deep V near the crown. Among caravan hawk-tamers, such a V-creased shell signals a chick about to pipe; jostle it and the peep will call the dam home at once. He cradled it, felt the faint beat, and decided it was the one; for a breath he thought the Arak more than a bird. Then the whisper of great wings neared and a shadow reached the ledge’s end.
implausible_knowledge: Among caravan hawk-tamers, such a V-creased shell signals a chick about to pipe; jostle it and the peep will call the dam home at once.
question: As the rush of great wings grows nearer while he holds the chosen egg, what will Waxman do?
answer_with_knowledge: Set the egg back gently and keep still in hiding, leaving empty-handed.
answer_without_knowledge: Clutch the egg and scramble for cover, trying to escape with it.
